title: Status application
principles: Status application depends on both status chance and the number of valid damage instances that can independently apply status.
Application frequency, multishot, pellet count, beam ticks, and repeated attacks may create more status opportunities, but only valid hits contribute in practice.
Status chance alone should not define the weapon's primary role; repeated applications must support the complete attack pattern.
Multishot creates additional projectiles or damage instances that may roll status independently; it does not directly increase the status chance of an existing projectile.
Innate pellet count creates more possible status rolls per trigger pull, but only pellets that connect contribute to practical status application.
Fire rate can increase status opportunities per second, but its practical value depends on magazine size, reload time, ammunition economy, and target contact.
High pellet count with low per-pellet status chance should not be treated as reliable when spread or accuracy prevents most pellets from connecting.
A weapon with fewer instances and higher status chance may apply effects more consistently per hit, while many instances may create more total applications when they connect.
Status chance above 100 percent can produce additional effects from the same eligible damage instance and should not be treated as wasted probability.
Direct hits, radial damage, beam ticks, chained damage, and other secondary events may use independent statistics or restrictions.
Do not estimate practical status output by multiplying status chance, fire rate, multishot, and pellet count without accounting for hit consistency, downtime, and mechanics.
Only discuss accuracy, spread, recoil, or target-contact limitations when those fields or mechanics are explicitly supplied.

aligned: Status chance and application frequency reinforce one another.
Repeated valid damage instances provide meaningful evidence for recurring application.
moderate: Status application can contribute consistently but does not necessarily define the primary role.
The supplied data supports a secondary or hybrid contribution.
mixed: The weapon creates many opportunities with limited chance per instance, or favorable chance with limited frequency.
Practical performance depends strongly on valid contact.
limited: The supplied data provides limited evidence for dependable status application.

exceptions: Do not assume beam tick frequency when it is not supplied.
Do not assume radial, chained, or secondary damage shares direct-hit status behavior.
Do not call the weapon a status weapon only because status chance is numerically higher.
